_meta.description: Phase 2 data patch Part 3 for distress registry 051: 20 new terms, primarily verbal forms in Hebrew and Greek covering actions of pressing, oppressing, grieving, confining, troubling, and related distress verbs.

rows.revision_note: Corrective patch — Phase 2 data previously absent despite mti_terms being populated

rows.meaning: to press, afflict, distress; metaphorically to trouble, afflict, distress
rows.lsj_entry: to press, squeeze; to press hard, oppress; passive, to be in straits

rows.meaning: to tire down with toil, exhaust with labour; to afflict, oppress, distress (Act 7:24; 2 Pet 2:7)
rows.lsj_entry: to wear out by toil; to oppress, distress

rows.meaning: to make sorrowful, to grieve; passive, to be grieved, to be in heaviness
rows.lsj_entry: to cause grief or distress; passive, to feel grief, be pained, distressed

rows.meaning: to flay, lacerate; to vex, annoy, trouble (Mt 9:36; Mk 5:35; Lk 7:6; 8:49)
rows.lsj_entry: to skin, flay; to harass, trouble, annoy

rows.meaning: to crowd into a narrow place; to be in a strait, to be pressed for room, to be straitened, distressed (2 Cor 4:8; 6:12)
rows.lsj_entry: to crowd into a narrow space; passive, to be straitened

rows.meaning: to hold together, hold fast; to constrain, press upon, oppress; to be seized or afflicted with disease or distress
rows.lsj_entry: to hold together; to constrain, compress; to beset, besiege; to be seized by pain or disease

rows.meaning:
1) to disturb, rout, make haste, be disturbed, be dismayed, be terrified, be hastened, be hurried
1a) (Niphal) to be disturbed, be dismayed, be terrified, be hurried, be anxious
1b) (Piel) to dismay, terrify
1c) (Pual) to be terrified, be hastened
1d) (Hiphil) to trouble, disturb

1) to press, drive, oppress, exact, exert demanding pressure
1a) (Qal) to press, drive hard, exact
1b) (Niphal) to be hard pressed

1) to hurt, pain, grieve
1a) (Qal) to hurt, pain
1b) (Niphal) to be in pain, be hurt, be grieved
1c) (Piel) to cause pain, wound
1d) (Hiphil) to cause pain
1e) (Hithpael) to feel pain, grieve

1) to constrain, press, bring into straits
1a) (Qal) to press, constrain
1b) (Hiphil) to oppress, press hard upon

1) to confine, cramp, besiege
1a) (Qal) to confine, shut in, besiege, bind
1b) (Hiphil) to make narrow, show distress
to show hostility to, be an adversary, vex, provoke
vexer, rival wife
to bind, be restricted, be in straits, be in distress
rows.status_note: tsa.rar H6887 appears under four distinct Strong's sub-entries (B,C,D,E) covering constrain/distress/vex/rival. Each extracted as separate term per source file headings.

rows.meaning: to bind, distress, be an adversary to
to show hostility toward, vex
to be a rival wife, to vex

1) to feel a loathing, abhor, be distressed
1a) (Qal) to feel a loathing, dread, abhor

1) to be hard, be severe, be fierce, be harsh
1a) (Qal) to be hard, be difficult, be severe
1b) (Niphal) to be difficult
1c) (Piel) to make hard, make difficult
1d) (Hiphil) to make hard, harden

1) to put forth, meditate, muse, commune, speak, complain, ponder, sing
1a) (Qal) to put forth
1b) (Polel) to meditate, muse, complain, talk, sing, speak

Hiphil/Piel: to cause dismay or terror in another
God grieves/is hurt (Gen 6:6; Isa 63:10)

Pain rooted in bodily experience; term covers both physical and inner pain
Hiphil: to oppress, press hard upon another

Hiphil: to cause hardness, harden another's neck/heart

rows.verse_text: For the gate is narrow and the way is hard that leads to life, and those who find it are few.
If we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; and if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which you experience when you patiently endure the same sufferings that we suffer.
We are afflicted in every way, but not crushed; perplexed, but not driven to despair;
For when we were with you, we kept telling you beforehand that we were to suffer affliction, just as it has come to pass, and just as you know.
And seeing one of them being wronged, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian.
and if he rescued righteous Lot, greatly distressed by the sensual conduct of the wicked
And they were very sorrowful and began to say to him one after another, Is it I, Lord?
Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
For I wrote to you out of much affliction and anguish of heart and with many tears, not to cause you pain but to let you know the abundant love that I have for you.
And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
While he was still speaking, there came from the ruler's house some who said, Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the Teacher any further?
And Jesus went with them. When he was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends, saying to him, Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you come under my roof.
We are afflicted in every way, but not crushed; perplexed, but not driven to despair;
You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted in your own affections.
And Jesus said, Who was it that touched me? When all denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds surround you and are pressing in on you!
I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how great is my distress until it is accomplished!
I am hard pressed between the two. My desire is to depart and be with Christ, for that is far better.
And Joseph said to his brothers, I am Joseph! Is my father still alive? But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed at his presence.
My soul also is greatly troubled. But you, O Lord— how long?
by your favor, O Lord, you made my mountain stand strong; you hid your face; I was dismayed.
Therefore my loins are filled with anguish; pangs have seized me, like the pangs of a woman in labor; I am bowed down so that I cannot hear; I am dismayed so that I cannot see.

On that day, declares the Lord, I will strike every horse with panic, and its rider with madness. But for the sake of the house of Judah I will keep my eyes open, when I strike every horse of the peoples with blindness.
Then the Lord said, I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters. I know their sufferings,
For the yoke of his burden, and the staff for his shoulder, the rod of his oppressor, you have broken as on the day of Midian.
He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth; like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth.
From him shall come the cornerstone, from him the tent peg, from him the battle bow, from him every ruler— all of them together.
And the Lord regretted that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him to his heart.

But David vowed again, saying, Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes, and he thinks, Do not let Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved. But truly, as the Lord lives and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death.

Then he said to them, Go your way. Eat the fat and drink sweet wine and send portions to anyone who has nothing ready, for this day is holy to our Lord. And do not be grieved, for the joy of the Lord is your strength.
But they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit; therefore he turned to be their enemy, and himself fought against them.
And the multitude of all the nations that fight against Ariel, all that fight against her and her stronghold and distress her, shall be like a dream, a vision of the night.

and have forgotten the Lord, your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth, and you fear continually all the day because of the wrath of the oppressor, when he sets himself to destroy? And where is the wrath of the oppressor?
But if it makes no peace with you, but makes war against you, then you shall besiege it.
Ben-hadad the king of Syria gathered all his army together. Thirty-two kings were with him, and horses and chariots. And he went up and closed in on Samaria and besieged it.
Blessed be the Lord, for he has wondrously shown his steadfast love to me when I was in a besieged city.
Harass the Midianites and strike them down,
And the Lord said to me, Do not harass Moab or contend with them in battle, for I will not give you any of their land for a possession, because I have given Ar to the people of Lot for a possession.
And her rival used to provoke her grievously to irritate her, because the Lord had closed her womb.

But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those of them whom you let remain shall be as barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides, and they shall trouble you in the land where you dwell.
Greatly have they afflicted me from my youth — let Israel now say —
But if you carefully obey his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
Then David said to Gad, I am in great distress. Let us fall into the hand of the Lord, for his mercy is great; but let me not fall into the hand of man.

for they have harassed you with their wiles, with which they beguiled you in the matter of Peor, and in the matter of Cozbi, the daughter of the chief of Midian, their sister, who was killed on the day of the plague on account of Peor.
For I know how many are your transgressions and how great are your sins — you who afflict the righteous, who take a bribe, and turn aside the needy in the gate.
And her rival used to provoke her grievously to irritate her, because the Lord had closed her womb.
Then Rebekah said to Isaac, I loathe my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women like these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?
But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and the more they spread abroad. And the Egyptians were in dread of the people of Israel.
And the people spoke against God and against Moses, Why have you brought us up out of Egypt to die in the wilderness? For there is no food and no water, and we loathe this worthless food.
But I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and though I multiply my signs and wonders in the land of Egypt,
Why should you harden your hearts as the Egyptians and Pharaoh hardened their hearts? After he had dealt severely with them, did they not send the people away, and they departed?
Blessed is the one who fears the Lord always, but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan, and he hears my voice.
when I remember God, I moan; when I meditate, my spirit faints. Selah
I pour out my complaint before him; I tell my trouble before him.
Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

rows.note: chul H2342A owned by registry 005 (anguish); XREF in distress Part 3
cha.rah H2734 owned by registry 004 (anger); XREF in distress Part 3 — to be incensed appears in distress source but analysis held in anger registry
a.tsav H6087A (to hurt/grieve) shares semantic overlap with grief registry terms; somatic-inner link pattern

rows.description: tsa.rar H6887 appears as four sub-entries (B/C/D/E: constrain/distress/vex/rival), all showing occurrence count 26. These likely share the same underlying verse pool. Consolidation review recommended at Phase 2 synthesis.
cha.rah H2734 (to be incensed): source file contains full extraction block but term is owned by registry 004 (anger). Treated as XREF per mti_term_cross_refs row id=30.
chul H2342A (to twist/tremble): source file contains full extraction block but term is owned by registry 005 (anguish). Treated as XREF per mti_term_cross_refs row id=24.
